display.purpose: Mobile hero statement only. Instrument Serif italic — the human, warm voice at the top of the page on small screens. Never used for UI chrome or headings.

headline.purpose: Page titles and case study titles. Instrument Sans 700 — decisive, structural. Tighter line-height (1.3) for short title strings.

title.purpose: Project entry titles, section headings, featured work labels. Weight 400 keeps them subordinate to Headline; negative tracking tightens display at wider sizes.

body.purpose: All prose content. Comfortable reading at 15–17px with 1.6–1.7 line-height. Case study text constrained to 860px max-width for comfortable measure.

label.purpose: Category labels, section headers, uppercase tag lines. Uppercase + 1.5px tracking gives visual weight at small size. Do not apply to running text.

mono.purpose: Typed.js hero terminal (desktop), structured metadata (dates, skill labels, case study credits, prev/next labels). High line-height (1.8) for breathing room at small sizes.

shadows.name: card
shadows.purpose: Resting state for featured work cards. Whisper-level depth. Separates card from page without decorative intent.

shadows.name: card-hover
shadows.purpose: Hover state for cards, paired with translateY(-3px). Reveals more presence in response to interaction.

shadows.name: nav
shadows.purpose: Mobile nav dropdown bottom edge hairline. Structural boundary marker, not decoration.

shadows.name: section
shadows.purpose: Inward top shadow separating featured work section from hero on desktop. Used once; negative spread collapses its reach.

motion.name: transition-fast
motion.purpose: Color-only transitions: link hover, icon color change. Snap-fast to feel responsive.

motion.name: transition-base
motion.purpose: State-change transitions: nav menu open/close, opacity shifts, transform transitions. Standard interaction rhythm.

motion.name: ease-card-hover
motion.purpose: Card lift and shadow change on hover. Fast exit, gradual entry.

motion.name: ease-reveal
motion.purpose: Content reveals and entrance animations. Used on case study header entrance (cs-header-enter) and image hover scale.

motion.name: ease-indicator
motion.purpose: Project entry hover indicator (scaleY spring). The only spring curve in the system — do not generalize.

breakpoints.name: tablet
breakpoints.purpose: Hero switches from typed.js terminal to mobile statement. Featured grid collapses to single column. Nav logo reduces.

breakpoints.name: mobile
breakpoints.purpose: Nav becomes hamburger dropdown with staggered item reveal.

breakpoints.name: small-mobile
breakpoints.purpose: Tight spacing adjustments for smallest viewports.

components.name: Featured Work Card
components.description: Case study / project card in the homepage featured work grid. Lifts on hover.
components.html:
Redesigning Sephora's Search
UX RESEARCH · INTERACTION DESIGN

How might a better search experience help customers find the right product without being overwhelmed by choice?
components.css: .ds-card { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 8px; background-color: #f8f9f7; border-radius: 12px; padding: 32px; box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.08); text-decoration: none; transition: transform 200ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 0, 0.2, 1), box-shadow 200ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-card:hover { transform: translateY(-3px); box-shadow: 0 4px 16px rgba(0,0,0,0.12); } .ds-card__title { font-family: 'Instrument Sans', Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; color: #4a4849; text-decoration: underline; text-underline-offset: 2px; transition: color 150ms ease; } .ds-card:hover .ds-card__title { color: #284c4c; } .ds-card__tags { font-family: 'Instrument Sans', Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 400; letter-spacing: 1px; text-transform: uppercase; color: #a45e5f; line-height: 1.5; } .ds-card__description { font-family: 'Instrument Sans', Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 15px; color: #4a4849; line-height: 1.6; margin: 0; }

components.name: Navigation Link
components.description: Desktop nav link in default and active states.
components.html:
• Work
  • About
components.css: .ds-nav__menu { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0; list-style: none; padding: 0; margin: 0; } .ds-nav__link { font-family: 'Instrument Sans', Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 15px; color: #4a4849; text-decoration: none; padding: 4px 16px; letter-spacing: 0.25px; line-height: 1.5; transition: color 150ms ease; display: block; } .ds-nav__link:hover { color: #666c6b; text-decoration: underline; text-underline-offset: 2px; } .ds-nav__link--active { color: #284c4c; font-weight: 700; } .ds-nav__link:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #284c4c; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: 4px; }

components.name: Project Entry
components.description: Numbered project list row on the projects page. Dims siblings on hover; reveals teal left indicator.
components.html:
• 01
    Weee! Usability Study UX RESEARCH
  • 02
    Redesigning Sephora INTERACTION DESIGN
components.css: .ds-project-list { list-style: none; padding: 0; margin: 0; } .ds-project-list--hovering .ds-project-entry { opacity: 0.2; } .ds-project-list--hovering .ds-project-entry--hovered { opacity: 1; } .ds-project-entry { border-bottom: 1px solid #e5e5e5; padding: 40px 0 40px 24px; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 24px; transition: opacity 300ms ease; position: relative; } .ds-project-entry:first-child { border-top: 1px solid #e5e5e5; } .ds-project-entry::before { content: ''; position: absolute; left: 0; top: 0; bottom: 0; width: 2px; background: #284c4c; transform: scaleY(0); transform-origin: center; transition: transform 250ms cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1); border-radius: 2px; } .ds-project-entry--hovered::before { transform: scaleY(1); } .ds-project-entry__num { font-family: 'Inconsolata', 'Courier New', monospace; font-size: 60px; font-weight: 400; color: #4a4849; opacity: 0.06; line-height: 1; flex-shrink: 0; width: 80px; letter-spacing: -1px; user-select: none; } .ds-project-entry__content { flex: 1; display: flex; align-items: baseline; gap: 24px; flex-wrap: wrap; } .ds-project-entry__title { font-family: 'Instrument Sans', Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: 400; color: #4a4849; text-decoration: none; transition: color 150ms ease; letter-spacing: -0.2px; } .ds-project-entry--hovered .ds-project-entry__title { color: #284c4c; } .ds-project-entry__tags { font-family: 'Instrument Sans', Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; letter-spacing: 1px; text-transform: uppercase; color: #a45e5f; flex-shrink: 0; }

components.name: Case Study Pill
components.description: Skill and method tags in case study headers. Mono, lightweight, no background fill.
components.html: User Research Figma Usability Testing
components.css: .ds-pill-group { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 4px; } .ds-pill { font-family: 'Inconsolata', 'Courier New', monospace; font-size: 12px; color: #666c6b; border: 1px solid #e5e5e5; border-radius: 20px; padding: 3px 10px; letter-spacing: 0.5px; line-height: 1.5; display: inline-block; }

components.name: Tag Label
components.description: Uppercase category / discipline tag in Dried Rose. Used on cards, project entries, and case study headers.
components.css: .ds-tag { font-family: 'Instrument Sans', Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 400; letter-spacing: 1px; text-transform: uppercase; color: #a45e5f; line-height: 1.5; }

components.name: Tooltip
components.description: Tippy.js 'tipbox' theme for homepage external link hover previews.
components.html: A real-time grocery delivery app with a loyal following in Asian-American communities
components.css: .ds-tooltip { background-color: #efefec; color: #4a4a4a; font-family: 'Instrument Sans', Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 13px; border-radius: 4px; padding: 8px 12px; box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.08); max-width: 220px; line-height: 1.5; }

narrative.overview:
This is a designer's accumulated work — each piece chosen, never padded. The system does not compete with the case studies inside it; it creates the conditions for them to land. The aesthetic logic is restraint as confidence: generous whitespace, a near-invisible neutral ground, and two carefully rationed accents that appear precisely when they're needed and nowhere else.

The type hierarchy is built on a single sans family (Instrument Sans) for structural clarity, with Instrument Serif reserved for moments that need a human touch — italic hero statements on mobile, bylines, and the odd section heading that benefits from warmth. Inconsolata handles structured meta: the typed.js terminal hero on desktop, date ranges, skill labels in case studies. Three families, each with a clear lane.

This system explicitly rejects the visual language of the existing Webflow site, overproduced SaaS landing pages, and the default portfolio template — the one with gradient hero text, glassmorphism cards, and identical icon-title-description rows.
keyCharacteristics: Two-accent restrained palette; teal is voice, rose is categorization signal
Fluid typography with a 15px–34px scale range; no flat steps
Cards rest flat; elevation appears only in response to interaction
Transitions are fast and directional (150ms–250ms, ease-out); no bounce
Accessibility-first: skip nav, focus rings, aria labels, reduced-motion support built into base styles

rules.name: The Still Water Rule
rules.body: Deep Still Water (#284c4c) appears on 10% or less of any given screen. It is an accent, not a theme color. Never use it as a background fill for content areas, section blocks, or card surfaces.

rules.name: The Two-Accent Rule
rules.body: Dried Rose is categorization; Deep Still Water is emphasis. They do not swap roles. A tag is never teal; an active nav link is never rose.

rules.name: The Serif as Whisper Rule
rules.body: Instrument Serif appears when a surface needs to feel inhabited, not designed. It is never used for UI chrome (buttons, labels, nav). If adding serif feels like decoration rather than warmth, use sans instead.

rules.name: The Mono as Structure Rule
rules.body: Inconsolata signals precision and meta-information, not personality. Do not use it for headings, CTAs, or marketing language. It belongs on data, dates, and the terminal aesthetic of the hero.

rules.name: The Whisper Lift Rule
rules.body: Shadows appear only in response to state (hover, overlay) or to mark a structural boundary between page zones. A surface at rest is flat. Never add shadow to reinforce visual hierarchy that spacing and color difference already establish.

dos: Do use Deep Still Water (#284c4c) only for interactive emphasis: active nav, link hover, focus rings, the project entry hover indicator.
Do use Dried Rose (#a45e5f) exclusively as a category and tag signal.
Do constrain case study body prose to the 860px content container for comfortable reading measure.
Do use the 250ms ease transition for state-change animations and 150ms for color-only transitions.
Do apply the focus ring on every interactive element: 2px solid Deep Still Water, outline-offset 3px, border-radius radius-sm.
Do let spacing carry hierarchy before reaching for type weight.
Do use Inconsolata for structured meta and Instrument Serif for editorial warmth — each family has a lane.
donts: Don't replicate the current Webflow site at jzsun.me — the new system is a deliberate evolution beyond that visual language.
Don't introduce gradient text (background-clip + gradient background). Solid color only.
Don't use glassmorphism (backdrop-filter, blur) as decorative treatment.
Don't add bounce easing or elastic springs outside the projects page entry indicator.
Don't use border-left or border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe on cards, callouts, or list items.
Don't reach for Storm Haze (#749eaf) as a default choice for new components.
Don't use all-caps body copy — uppercase is reserved for short labels and category tags at 12–13px.
